Title: LinkedIn Learning Webinar
Description:
The libraries are pleased to announce this week’s webinar on ‘LinkedIn Learning’ (formerly known as Lynda.com). 

LinkedIn Learning has over 15,000 classes available, covering education, technology, creative skills, business, and many more. 

Access is free for all KU faculty, staff, and students. Attend the webinar to learn about the latest features of LinkedIn Learning with a demonstration of how to access and get the most out of this learning tool.  

Please use the below URL to join the session online (MS TEAMS):

https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_MGI0MWJkMjgtNDZiOS00ZDNhLTg4YTMtNjMzN2RjNmE0Mzdk%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2208fe1c0a-19f5-4f24-a662-fdd5dd460025%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%223c1587fc-a30b-4421-82eb-abf0b1c74451%22%7d

Title: PubMed Webinar
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize webinar on ‘PubMed’. 

PubMed is a freely-available research tool from the National Library of Medicine in the US. It includes the MEDLINE database, which indexes millions of journal citations and abstracts in areas including medicine, nursing, and much more. This workshop will cover the basics of using PubMed, including its new search interface. Please join in to learn more about this valuable database.

Please use the below URL to join the session online (MS TEAMS):

Title: EMBASE Training Session
Description:
In collaboration with Elsevier, the Libraries are pleased to organize a training session on trial database EMBASE. It will be delivered by Mr. Piotr Golkiewicz, Customer Consultant in Life Science Solutions for Elsevier.

 The training outline


	Introduction to Embase - content and functionalities
	Quick Search functionality - searches using various fields and logical operators
	Using filters
	Description of Embase record
	Searches using triple-linking functionality
	PICO search
	PV search
	Medical Device search
	Emtree search
	Literature search
	Journal search
	Managing the search results
	Q&A

Title: Web of Science Workshop by Clarivate
Description:
As part of our commitment to support you in your academic work, the KU Libraries in collaboration with Clarivate will be hosting a workshop on ‘Web of Science’.

(WoS) provides access to authoritative content from the highest impact journals in science, social sciences, arts, and humanities. 

This session will overview WoS, focusing on research and publishing. Participants will learn how to use the power of WoS, using citations to identify the most recent research and analyzing results to identify authors, funding agencies, and journals.


	Identify the most recent and influential publications
	Use citations to establish links between publications, authors, and subjects
	Identify key opinion leaders and find potential collaborators




Journal Citation Reports (JCR) offers a systematic, objective means to critically evaluate the world's leading journals, with quantifiable, statistical information based on citation data. This session will also cover the basics of Journals citations metrics such as the Journal Impact Factor - what are they, and how are they created. 


	Navigation overview – what’s new for getting around 
	Ways to find a journal profile
	What are the top journals in my field?
	Compare journals 


Please use the below URL to join the session (MS TEAMS):

https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NjQyZTM0NmQtOThjMy00ZDQ2LWIxODYtM2I4M2U0OTVjYjg3%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2208fe1c0a-19f5-4f24-a662-fdd5dd460025%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%223c1587fc-a30b-4421-82eb-abf0b1c74451%22%7d

As part of our commitment to support you in your academic work, the KU Libraries in collaboration with Clarivate will be hosting a workshop on ‘Web of Science’.

(WoS) provides access to authoritative content from the highest impact journals in science, social sciences, arts, and humanities. 

This session will overview WoS, focusing on research and publishing. Participants will learn how to use the power of WoS, using citations to identify the most recent research and analyzing results to identify authors, funding agencies, and journals.


	Identify the most recent and influential publications
	Use citations to establish links between publications, authors, and subjects
	Identify key opinion leaders and find potential collaborators




Journal Citation Reports (JCR) offers a systematic, objective means to critically evaluate the world's leading journals, with quantifiable, statistical information based on citation data. This session will also cover the basics of Journals citations metrics such as the Journal Impact Factor - what are they, and how are they created. 


	Navigation overview – what’s new for getting around 
	Ways to find a journal profile
	What are the top journals in my field?
	Compare journals

Title: Web of Science Workshop by Clarivate
Description:
As part of our commitment to support you in your academic work, the KU Libraries in collaboration with Clarivate will be hosting a workshop on ‘Web of Science’.

(WoS) provides access to authoritative content from the highest impact journals in science, social sciences, arts, and humanities. 

This session will overview WoS, focusing on research and publishing. Participants will learn how to use the power of WoS, using citations to identify the most recent research and analyzing results to identify authors, funding agencies, and journals.


	Identify the most recent and influential publications
	Use citations to establish links between publications, authors, and subjects
	Identify key opinion leaders and find potential collaborators




Journal Citation Reports (JCR) offers a systematic, objective means to critically evaluate the world's leading journals, with quantifiable, statistical information based on citation data. This session will also cover the basics of Journals citations metrics such as the Journal Impact Factor - what are they, and how are they created.

The Libraries are pleased to announce the ‘Author Workshop’ this week. It will be delivered by Dr. Yasmin Amr Abdelraouf, Customer Research and Analytic Consultant for Elsevier.

Topics covered include:


	Academic Research
	Mystery of publishing
	Research Process
	Editorial & peer-review process 
	Tips for the basic full article structure
	Easy reference storing and citing with Mendeley 
	Increasing Your Academic Visibility & Impact

Title: LinkedIn Learning Webinar by the Publisher
RefWorks-Citation Management Workshop
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a workshop on RefWorks - Citation Management Tool. 

RefWorks is a citation management service that supports the needs of students, faculty, and researchers. With an improved user experience, full-text management, and collaboration features, RefWorks gives students and faculty a tool that enables a more efficient and reliable process for producing research papers.

Title: Webinar on 'How to Prepare a Successful Paper' by Springer-Nature
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a webinar on the “Author Workshop” in collaboration with Springer-Nature. The session will be delivered by Nathalie Jacobs, Executive Editor, Applied Sciences.

You will be offered:


	Invaluable advice on ways to make communicating the fruits of research as successful as possible, from writing a paper and choosing a journal to write a cover letter and responding to the comments of referees.
	An overview of the submission and peer review workflow, as well as the importance of research integrity and ethics.
	The post-publication aspects that need to be considered in order for a paper to have maximum impact.
	The chance for Questions & Answers


To register, please use this URL:  https://attendee.gotowebinar.com/register/4894544602045449739

After registering for the session, you will receive a confirmation email that includes the webinar link.

Title: Scopus & SciVal Workshop by Elsevier
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a workshop on ‘Scopus & SciVal’ by Elsevier. It will be delivered by Mr. Wael Mansour, Regional Customer Consultant for Elsevier. 

Topics covered include:

Scopus
•    Check Article-Level Metrics
•    Author Metrics and profile Editor
•    Journal level Metrics
•    How to select the right journal for you (as a researcher) in Scopus?

SciVal
•    How can you check your research strengths in SciVal as a researcher?
•    How to identify areas to improve?
•    How evaluate the current collaborations and to select the right partner?
•    How to check a topic and the rising stars in this topic?

The Libraries are pleased to organize a workshop on ‘Scopus & SciVal’ by Elsevier. It will be delivered by Mr. Wael Mansour, Regional Customer Consultant for Elsevier. 

Topics covered include:

Scopus
•    Check Article-Level Metrics
•    Author Metrics and profile Editor
•    Journal level Metrics
•    How to select the right journal for you (as a researcher) in Scopus?

SciVal
•    How can you check your research strengths in SciVal as a researcher?
•    How to identify areas to improve?
•    How evaluate the current collaborations and to select the right partner?
•    How to check a topic and the rising stars in this topic?

Please use the below URL to join the session (MS TEAMS):

https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_ZjFhMTQwYTUtMmVhOS00ZTJhLTk2YjAtNWUyODY3NDVlMDQy%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2208fe1c0a-19f5-4f24-a662-fdd5dd460025%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%223c1587fc-a30b-4421-82eb-abf0b1c74451%22%7d

Title: ORCiD & Research Impact
Description:
Khalifa University of Science and Technology has acquired an institutional ORCiD account to facilitate tracking faculty publications and generating various reports. ORCiD is a non-profit organization founded to assist researchers in tracking their publications, research awards, and other research activity by providing a single ID that operates across many platforms such as Scopus or Web of Science. In order for this effort to be successful, all faculty are requested to register for an ORCiD and connect it with Khalifa University. The goal of this exercise is to broaden the visibility of your work as a researcher, and of the University as a whole.

Title: Book Discussion - Ikigai: The Japanese Secret to a Long and Happy Life by Hector Gracia and Francesc Millares
Description:
Read the book and join us for a book discussion on MS TEAMS.

According to the Japanese, everyone has an ikigai—a reason for living. And according to the residents of the Japanese village with the world’s longest-living people, finding it is the key to a happier and longer life. Having a strong sense of ikigai—where what you love, what you’re good at, what you can get paid for, and what the world needs all overlap—means that each day is infused with meaning. It’s the reason we get up in the morning. It’s also the reason many Japanese never really retire. They remain active and work at what they enjoy, because they’ve found a real purpose in life—the happiness of always being busy. Source: Amazon.com
Location: 1st Floor Reading Area, Main Campus Library

Title: Medical Clinical Resources
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a webinar on the “Medical Clinical Resources”.

You will be introduced to some of the resources available to you to assist in working with information needs in clinical and point-of-care settings. These resources can help inform medical decisions while working with patients. UpToDate, Lexicomp, and other information sources will be discussed.

The URL of the session will be shared soon, stay tuned!

Title: Medical Clinical Resources
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a webinar on the “Medical Clinical Resources”.

You will be introduced to some of the resources available to you to assist in working with information needs in clinical and point-of-care settings. These resources can help inform medical decisions while working with patients. UpToDate, Lexicomp, and other information sources will be discussed.

The URL of the session will be shared soon, stay tuned!

Title: Medical Evidence Based Practice Resources
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a webinar on the “Medical Evidence Based Practice Resources”. 

This webinar will help you to use the library’s resources to find information related to Evidence-Based Practice (EBP) and Evidence-Based Medicine (EBM). EBM is the use of current evidence in making decisions about the care of individual patients. EBM integrates an individual’s clinical experience and patient values with the best available research information. The webinar will introduce library databases and freely-available resources used in finding current research information.

Please use the below link to join the session (MS TEAMS):

https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_YzhlNGMwZjUtM2Y4My00ODlhLWJhZTItYmFkZWY2NWMwY2Qw%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2208fe1c0a-19f5-4f24-a662-fdd5dd460025%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%223c1587fc-a30b-4421-82eb-abf0b1c74451%22%7d

Title: Patents & Patentability Workshop by Patents Office
Description:
The Libraries are pleased to organize a workshop on “Patents & Patentability”.



Do you want to know about Patents and Patentability, some practical tips to search patent databases? This workshop will help you to:


	Overview of patents and patentability
	Identifying types of Intellectual Property (IP)
	Patent databases – how to conduct a search  
	Understanding (basic) patent classification
	Protecting and patenting your inventions at KU




Get advice from experts at KU: Winsom A. Dunn - Manager, Intellectual Property Agreements and Dr. Pradeep Doggali, Senior Technology Portfolio Specialist

Please use the following URL to join the session (MS TEAMS):

https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NmQxOWJjMjktZDYwMy00MmFjLTgyODQtYTgyNGMwN2E4MTRi%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2208fe1c0a-19f5-4f24-a662-fdd5dd460025%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%223c1587fc-a30b-4421-82eb-abf0b1c74451%22%7d

Do you want to know about Patents and Patentability, some practical tips to search patent databases? This workshop will help you to:


	Overview of patents and patentability
	Identifying types of Intellectual Property (IP)
	Patent databases – how to conduct a search  
	Understanding (basic) patent classification
	Protecting and patenting your inventions at KU




Get advice from experts at KU: Winsom A. Dunn - Manager, Intellectual Property Agreements and Dr. Pradeep Doggali, Senior Technology Portfolio Specialist

Please use the following URL to join the session (MS TEAMS):

https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NmQxOWJjMjktZDYwMy00MmFjLTgyODQtYTgyNGMwN2E4MTRi%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%2208fe1c0a-19f5-4f24-a662-fdd5dd460025%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%223c1587fc-a30b-4421-82eb-abf0b1c74451%22%7d

Title: Open Access Session for Master & PhD students
Description:
Dear Students,

The Library is pleased to invite you to attend the KU Open Access (OA) program session, organized for you next week.

Topics covered include:

•    How the KU OA program works
•    What the guidelines are for getting your publishing fees paid for by the university
•    How using the OA publishing model can help you get more recognition for your scholarly work
•    Answering any questions that you might have

•    How the KU OA program works
•    What the guidelines are for getting your publishing fees paid for by the university
•    How using the OA publishing model can help you get more recognition for your scholarly work
•    Answering any questions that you might have

Title: LinkedIn Learning
Description:
The libraries are pleased to organize a workshop on ‘LinkedIn Learning’ (formerly known as Lynda.com).

LinkedIn Learning has over 15,000 classes available, covering education, technology, creative skills, business, and many more.

Access is free for all KU faculty, staff, and students. Attend the webinar to learn about the latest features of LinkedIn Learning with a demonstration of how to access and get the most out of this learning tool.

Title: E-resources & Search Techniques
Description:
The library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘E-Resources and Search Techniques’ workshop. 





Attend the session and learn to:


	Use KU subscribed databases and academic resources.
	Make the best use of them for your research and academic success. 
	Use tips and tricks to narrow your search using Boolean logic and truncations. 
	Organize your search results and export them to citation managers.

Title: Web of Science Workshop
Description:
The Library is pleased to invite you to attend the 'Web of Science' workshop.

The Web of Science™ is the world’s most trusted publisher-independent global citation database. Guided by the legacy of Dr Eugene Garfield, inventor of the world’s first citation index, the Web of Science is the most powerful research engine delivering researchers and librarians with best-in-class publication and citation data for confident discovery, access and assessment.



In this workshop we will learn how to:


	Access Web of Science
	Study topic background using citation network
	Find the most influential papers in any domain
	Determine the key opinion leaders in your field
	Create a researcher profile and track your scientific impact using the highest quality data.

Guided by the legacy of Dr Eugene Garfield, inventor of the world’s first citation index, the Web of Science is the most powerful research engine delivering researchers and librarians with best-in-class publication and citation data for confident discovery, access and assessment.



In this workshop we will learn how to:


	Access Web of Science
	Study topic background using citation network
	Find the most influential papers in any domain
	Determine the key opinion leaders in your field
	Create a researcher profile and track your scientific impact using the highest quality data.

Title: RefWorks -  Citation Management Tool
Description:
The Library is pleased to organize a workshop on RefWorks - Citation Management Tool. 

RefWorks is a citation management service that supports the needs of students, faculty, and researchers. With an improved user experience, full-text management, and collaboration features, RefWorks gives students and faculty a tool that enables a more efficient and reliable process for producing research papers.

Title: Open Access Awareness Day Event
Description:
The KU Library is organizing an Open Access Awareness Day event on Wednesday, 5th of October.
The event aims to highlight the benefits of publishing papers via the Open Access platform, which reflects how digital technology is changing the future of scientific publishing. The session will also showcase the influence of the OA on academic literature, and the university in general, as well as the existing agreements between Khalifa University and the publishers Wiley, IEEE, ACS, and PLOS.
Location: Main Campus, R Building - 1st floor, Auditorium (R01013)

Title: PubMed Workshop
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘PubMed’ workshop.

PubMed is a freely-available research tool from the National Library of Medicine in the US. It includes the MEDLINE database, which indexes millions of journal citations and abstracts in areas including medicine, nursing, and much more. This workshop will cover the basics of using PubMed, including its new search interface. Please join us to learn more about this valuable database.

Title: Author Workshop - How to publish your research paper by Wiley
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘Author Workshop’ by Wiley.

Topics covered include:


	What is open access and what are the benefits.
	Author workflow: How to submit a manuscript to a Wiley hybrid or fully gold open-access journal and get the APC covered through an existing agreement with your institution.
	Publication tips for authors:



	Why publish?
	Submission and review: What to expect?
	Writing tips
	Tools for authors: Author Services and Wiley Editing Services

Title: ORCiD & Research Impact
Description:
ORCiD & Research Impact: ORCiD is a non-profit organization founded to assist researchers in tracking their publications, research awards, and other research activity by providing a single ID that operates across many platforms such as Scopus or Web of Science. In order for this effort to be successful, all faculty are requested to register for an ORCiD and connect it with Khalifa University. The goal of this exercise is to broaden the visibility of your work as a researcher, and of the University as a whole.

Title: Open Access for Authors - Q & A session
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘Open Access Q&A’ session. 





Did you know that KU has a program to support Open Access publishing? Want to know more about how to get support for publishing in Open Access journals? 

Walter Hall will give you the details of the KU OA program and answer all your questions about this important topic.

Title: Open Access for Authors - Q & A session
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘Open Access Q&A’ session. 





Did you know that KU has a program to support Open Access publishing? Want to know more about how to get support for publishing in Open Access journals? 

Walter Hall will give you the details of the KU OA program and answer all your questions about this important topic.

Title: Patents & Patentability Webinar
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘Patents & Patentability’ webinar.



Topics covered include:


	Overview of patents and patentability
	Identifying types of Intellectual Property (IP)
	Patent databases – how to conduct a search  
	Understanding (basic) patent classification
	Protecting and patenting your inventions at KU






Get advice from experts at KU: Winsome A. Dunn - Manager, Intellectual Property & Agreements, and Dr. Pradeep Doggali, Senior Technology Portfolio Specialist

Title: Patents & Standards
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the ‘Patents and standards’ workshop. 

Patents and Standards are an inevitable source of information for research and innovation.

This workshop covers: 


	Overview of patents and patentability
	Identifying types of Intellectual Property (IP)
	Patent databases – how to conduct a search
	Importance of Engineering standards
	Explore standards databases 
	Discover practices for how to use refinements to get more relevant search results

Title: EMBASE and Cochrane Library Workshop
Description:
KU Library organized a one-hour session to introduce you to these two new E-resources, with an emphasis on Embase.

Embase is a biomedical database that covers general medicine, drugs and pharmacology, medical devices, clinical medicine, and more. Along with PubMed, it is a core research database in the medical sciences. It includes its own set of subject terms, Emtree, to help you build focused searches.

The Cochrane Library consists of a collection of evidence-based healthcare databases with a focus on systematic reviews and meta-analyses of existing research. It is designed to provide evidence to support healthcare decision-making and to inform those receiving care.

Title: Book Discussion - Atomic Habits: An Easy & Proven Way To Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones By James Clear
Description:
Read the book to join us for a discussion.

If you're having trouble changing habits, the problem isn't you, it is your system.  In Atomic Habits, James Clear, a leading expert on habits, reveals practical strategies that teach how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. Clear is known for his ability to distill complex topics into simple behaviors that can be easily applied to daily life and work. Readers will be inspired and entertained with true stories from Olympic gold medalists, award-winning artists, business leaders, life-saving physicians, and star comedians who have used the science of small habits to master their craft and vault to the top of their field. Amazon.com 

 

**There are a very limited number of copies of this book available. Please see your Librarian, Muna Abdulla at the Main Campus Library**
Location: 1st Floor Reading Area, Main Campus Library

Title: ProQuest Central Workshop
Description:
KU Library is pleased to invite you to attend the "ProQuest Central" workshop. 

ProQuest Central is one of the largest aggregated databases available in the market today. 

Topics covered include:


	Identify the focus and scope of ProQuest Central
	Recognize the importance of multidisciplinary resources
	Use the ProQuest platform to locate journals, theses, magazines, and more content types of use in research and in teaching

Title: Medical Clinical Resources Workshop
Description: You will be introduced to some of the resources available to you to assist in working with information needs in clinical and point-of-care settings. These resources can help inform medical decisions while working with patients. UpToDate, Lexicomp, and other information sources will be discussed.
